result.summary: The page has a few visual defects: an empty call-to-action button in the header, a 'SCROLL DOWN' overlay that overlaps and obscures the legal terms text, and very small fine print that is partially unreadable. Overall branding and promo layout look intact.

issues.description: The prominent yellow button in the top-right of the header is completely empty — it has no label (likely intended to be 'Sign Up' or 'Join Now'), so users cannot tell what the primary CTA does.
issues.suggestion: Ensure the button label text renders (e.g., 'Sign Up' / 'Log In'). Check for a failed text asset load, missing localization string, or CSS hiding the label.

issues.description: The 'SCROLL DOWN' pill/overlay is positioned directly on top of the legal terms & conditions paragraph, hiding several lines of required disclosure text behind it.
issues.suggestion: Reposition the scroll indicator below or above the fine-print block, or add spacing/z-index and padding so the T&C text is fully visible — legal copy must not be obscured.

issues.description: The fine-print terms text is rendered in very small dark-blue text on a saturated yellow background and is partially cut off/overlapped, making it extremely hard to read.
issues.suggestion: Increase the font size of the legal text, improve contrast, and ensure the full paragraph is rendered without truncation or overlap.

issues.description: The two promo columns at the bottom ('ALL PLAYERS' / 'NEW PLAYERS' badges) are vertically misaligned by a few pixels, and the poker bonus dollar amount ($1,000) appears to nearly touch the column edge.
issues.suggestion: Align the two badge/headline blocks to a shared baseline and add consistent horizontal padding so both promo cards look balanced.

issues.description: The hamburger menu icon in the header has uneven line lengths/offsets, giving it a slightly broken or unintentional appearance.
issues.suggestion: Verify the hamburger icon asset renders as designed; if the staggered lines are intentional branding, ensure consistent spacing and alignment.
